feedback.text: Thank you for launching this initiative. In addition to what is being discussed in the Digital Fairness Act, I would like to take further account of the possibility of banning certain companies from removing video games regularly purchased by the end user (in addition without any compensation). I find it important to emphasise that a user who has regularly purchased software is actually the owner of the software and is able to dispose of it to the extent he wishes, can own, delete or modify it as he wishes. This will counteract dubious morality practices such as planned obsolescience or arbitrary censorship. Thanks.
